actions-list.actionItem: The House proceeded with further one minute speeches.
Mr. Thompson (PA) asked unanimous consent that when the House adjourns today, it adjourn to meet at 9:00 a.m. on July 16. Agreed to without objection.
Motion to reconsider laid on the table Agreed to without objection.

actions-list.header: On passage Passed by the Yeas and Nays: 217
On motion to recommit Failed by the Yeas and Nays: 209

actions-list.actionItem: The previous question on the motion to recommit was ordered pursuant to clause 2(b) of rule XIX.
Mr. Amo moved to recommit to the Committee on Appropriations.
The House adopted the amendments en gros as agreed to by the Committee of the Whole House on the state of the Union.
The previous question was ordered pursuant to the rule.
The House rose from the Committee of the Whole House on the state of the Union to report H.R. 8595.

actions-list.header: On agreeing to the Roy amendment; Failed by recorded vote: 136
On agreeing to the Massie amendment; Failed by recorded vote: 6
On agreeing to the Massie amendment; Failed by recorded vote: 104
actions-list.actionItem: 314, 10 Present (Roll no. 243).

actions-list.header: On agreeing to the Fine amendment; Failed by recorded vote: 178
On agreeing to the Boebert amendment; Failed by recorded vote: 79

actions-list.actionItem: The House resolved into Committee of the Whole House on the state of the Union for further consideration.

Considered as unfinished business. H.R. 8595 — "Making appropriations for national security, Department of State, and related programs for the fiscal year ending September 30, 2027, and for other purposes."
The House convened, returning from a recess continuing the legislative day of July 15.
The Speaker announced that the House do now recess. The next meeting is subject to the call of the Chair.
Committee of the Whole House on the state of the Union rises leaving H.R. 8595 as unfinished business.
On motion that the committee rise Agreed to by voice vote.
Mr. Diaz-Balart moved that the committee rise.
On agreeing to the Tenney amendment; Agreed to by voice vote.
Pursuant to the provisions of H. Res. 1423, the Committee of the Whole proceeded with 10 minutes of debate on the Tenney amendment No. 28.

An amendment, offered by Ms. Tenney,  numbered 28 printed in Part A of House Report 119-749 to increase and decrease funding for the U.S. Commission on International Religious Freedom by $1M to emphasize the importance of ensuring religious freedom and equal access for all with respect to the Temple Mount.
On agreeing to the Steube amendment; Agreed to by voice vote.
Pursuant to the provisions of H. Res. 1423, the Committee of the Whole proceeded with 10 minutes of debate on the Steube amendment No. 27.

An amendment, offered by Mr. Steube,  numbered 27 printed in Part A of House Report 119-749 to increase withholding of assistance to Nigeria from 50% to 100%, keeping metrics in place that force Nigeria to take effective steps to prevent and respond to violence in the country.

At the conclusion of debate on the Roy amendment No. 20, the Chair put the question on agreeing to the amendment and by voice vote, announced that the ayes had prevailed. Ms. Lois Frankel (FL) demanded a recorded vote and the Chair postponed further proceedings until a time to be announced.
Pursuant to the provisions of H. Res. 1423, the Committee of the Whole proceeded with 10 minutes of debate on the Roy amendment No. 20.
An amendment, offered by Mr. Roy,  numbered 20 printed in Part A of House Report 119-749 to prohibit any funds from being made available to the United Nations.

At the conclusion of debate on the Massie amendment No. 9, the Chair put the question on agreeing to the amendment and by voice vote, announced that the noes had prevailed. Mr. Massie demanded a recorded vote and the Chair postponed further proceedings until a time to be announced.
Pursuant to the provisions of H. Res. 1423, the Committee of the Whole proceeded with 10 minutes of debate on the Massie amendment No. 9.

An amendment, offered by Mr. Massie,  numbered 9 printed in Part A of House Report 119-749 to prohibit funds appropriated by the Act from being used for Jordan, and accordingly reduces the National Security Investment Programs, the Foreign Military Financing Program, the Global Health Programs, and other programs accounts, collectively, by $2,100,000,000.

At the conclusion of debate on the Massie amendment No. 8, the Chair put the question on agreeing to the amendment and by voice vote, announced that the noes had prevailed. Mr. Massie demanded a recorded vote and the Chair postponed further proceedings until a time to be announced.
Pursuant to the provisions of H. Res. 1423, the Committee of the Whole proceeded with 10 minutes of debate on the Massie amendment No. 8.

An amendment, offered by Mr. Massie,  numbered 8 printed in Part A of House Report 119-749 to prohibit funds appropriated by the Act from being used for Israel, and accordingly reduces the Foreign Military Financing Program account by $3,300,000,000.

At the conclusion of debate on the Fine amendment No. 6, the Chair put the question on agreeing to the amendment and by voice vote, announced that the ayes had prevailed. Ms. Lois Frankel (FL) demanded a recorded vote and the Chair postponed further proceedings until a time to be announced.
Pursuant to the provisions of H. Res. 1423, the Committee of the Whole proceeded with 10 minutes of debate on the Fine amendment No. 6.
An amendment, offered by Mr. Fine,  numbered 6 printed in Part A of House Report 119-749 to strike the $139,575,000 for the Global Environment Facility.
On agreeing to the Crane amendment; Agreed to by voice vote.
Pursuant to the provisions of H. Res. 1423, the Committee of the Whole proceeded with 10 minutes of debate on the Crane amendment No. 4.

An amendment, offered by Mr. Crane,  numbered 4 printed in Part A of House Report 119-749 to require nations to pay the U.S. for military training exercise rather than receive them as a grant or at no-cost.

At the conclusion of debate on the Boebert amendment No. 1, the Chair put the question on agreeing to the amendment and by voice vote, announced that the noes had prevailed. Ms. Boebert demanded a recorded vote and the Chair postponed further proceedings until a time to be announced.
Pursuant to the provisions of H. Res. 1423, the Committee of the Whole proceeded with 10 minutes of debate on the Boebert amendment No. 1.
An amendment, offered by Ms. Boebert,  numbered 1 printed in Part A of House Report 119-749 to eliminate funding for the Fulbright Program.
On agreeing to the Diaz-Balart amendments; Agreed to by voice vote.
Pursuant to the provisions of H. Res. 1423, the Committee of the Whole proceeded with 20 minutes of debate on the Diaz-Balart amendment en bloc.
Amendments en bloc  offered by Mr. Diaz-Balart,  comprised of the following amendments printed in Part A of House Report 119-749 as en bloc: Nos. 3, 7, 17, 18, 21, 24, 25, 26, and 29.
The Committee of the Whole proceeded with one hour of general debate on H.R. 8595.
The Speaker designated the Honorable Jimmy Patronis to act as Chairman of the Committee.
House resolved itself into the Committee of the Whole House on the state of the Union pursuant to H. Res. 1423 and Rule XVIII.

Rule provides for consideration of H.R. 139, H.R. 8595, H.R. 9237 and H.R. 1181. The resolution provides for consideration of H.R. 139, H.R. 9237, and H.R. 1181 under a closed rule, and H.R. 8595 under a structured rule with one hour of debate and one motion to reconsider on each bill.

Considered under the provisions of rule H. Res. 1423. H.R. 8595 — "Making appropriations for national security, Department of State, and related programs for the fiscal year ending September 30, 2027, and for other purposes."
Motion to reconsider laid on the table Agreed to without objection.
On motion to suspend the rules and pass the bill Agreed to by voice vote.
The House proceeded with forty minutes of debate on H.R. 4288.
Considered under suspension of the rules.

Mr. Bost moved to suspend the rules and pass the bill. H.R. 4288 — "To name the Department of Veterans Affairs community-based outpatient clinic in San Jose, California, as the "Corporal Patrick D. Tillman VA Clinic"."
Motion to reconsider laid on the table Agreed to without objection.
On motion to suspend the rules and pass the bill Agreed to by voice vote.
The House proceeded with forty minutes of debate on H.R. 4647.
Considered under suspension of the rules.

Mr. Bost moved to suspend the rules and pass the bill. H.R. 4647 — "To designate the medical center of the Department of Veterans Affairs in West Haven, Connecticut, as the "Captain Paul W. 'Bud' Bucha VA Medical Center"."
The House received a message from the Senate. The Senate passed S. 2120 and agreed to S. Res. 801.
Motion to reconsider laid on the table Agreed to without objection.
On motion to suspend the rules and pass the bill Agreed to by voice vote.
The House proceeded with forty minutes of debate on H.R. 6963.
Considered under suspension of the rules.

Mr. Bost moved to suspend the rules and pass the bill. H.R. 6963 — "To designate the community-based outpatient clinic of the Department of Veterans Affairs in Lafayette, Louisiana, as the "Rodney C. Hamilton Sr. VA Clinic"."

At the conclusion of debate, the chair put the question on the motion to suspend the rules. Mr. Bost objected to the vote on the grounds that a quorum was not present. Further proceedings on the motion were postponed. The point of no quorum was considered as withdrawn.
The House proceeded with forty minutes of debate on H.R. 5362.
Considered under suspension of the rules.

Mr. Bost moved to suspend the rules and pass the bill. H.R. 5362 — "To name the Department of Veterans Affairs multispecialty clinic in Marietta, Georgia, as the "Colonel Michael H. Boyce Department of Veterans Affairs Multispecialty Clinic"."
Motion to reconsider laid on the table Agreed to without objection.
On motion to suspend the rules and pass the bill Agreed to by voice vote.
The House proceeded with forty minutes of debate on H.R. 6007.
Considered under suspension of the rules.

Mr. Bost moved to suspend the rules and pass the bill. H.R. 6007 — "To name the Department of Veterans Affairs community-based outpatient clinic in Newton, New Jersey, as the "Anthony 'Tony' J. Gallopo VA Clinic"."
The Speaker announced that votes on suspensions, if ordered, will be postponed until a time to be announced.
The House proceeded with one minute speeches which by direction of the Chair, would be limited to 15 per side of the aisle.
The Chair designated Mr. Baird to lead the Members in reciting the Pledge of Allegiance to the Flag.
The Speaker announced approval of the Journal.  Pursuant to clause 1, rule I, the Journal stands approved.
Today's prayer was offered by the House Chaplain, Chaplain Margaret Grun Kibben.
The House convened, starting a new legislative day.
